users.name: Alice Green
users.bio: Minimalist lifestyle enthusiast. Fitzroy local for 8 years.

users.name: Bob River
users.bio: Swap party organiser. Dad of two. Brunswick born and bred.

users.name: Maya Chen
users.bio: Moved to Melbourne from Shanghai 3 years ago. Love connecting with neighbours.

users.name: Margaret O'Brien
users.bio: Retired teacher. Downsizing after 40 years in the same house. It's harder than I thought!

users.name: Sam Nguyen
users.bio: Student at RMIT. Trying to furnish my apartment without buying new.

users.name: Priya Sharma
users.bio: Mum of three. We outgrow things faster than I can keep up! Grateful for this community.

exchangeItems.title: Vintage leather bag
exchangeItems.description: Used twice, great condition. Happy to swap for something useful or give to a neighbour who'd love it.

exchangeItems.title: Stack of design books (5)
exchangeItems.description: UX/UI design books that changed how I think. Free to whoever wants them — hoping they inspire someone the way they inspired me.

exchangeItems.title: Bamboo kitchen utensils set
exchangeItems.description: Complete set, barely used. Would love to swap for a nice plant or some homemade jam!

exchangeItems.title: Children's books bundle (20+)
exchangeItems.description: My kids have outgrown these. Ages 3-8, all in great condition. Would love them to go to a family who'll enjoy them as much as we did.

exchangeItems.title: Three warm winter coats (M/L)
exchangeItems.description: Downsizing my wardrobe. These are quality coats with years of life left. Stay warm, neighbours!

exchangeItems.title: IKEA standing desk frame
exchangeItems.description: Works perfectly, just redecorated. Free pickup from Collingwood. Would hate to see it go to landfill.

exchangeItems.title: Le Creuset casserole dish
exchangeItems.description: Wedding gift we never used (we got two!). This is the real deal, not a knockoff. Would swap for anything kitchen-related or give to someone who cooks.

exchangeItems.title: Textbooks: Design Thinking & HCI
exchangeItems.description: Finished these subjects, don't need them anymore. Save yourself $200 and pick them up from Brunswick East.

exchangeItems.title: Vintage sewing machine (Singer)
exchangeItems.description: My mother's Singer from the 60s. Still works beautifully. I can't bear to throw it away but I have no space. Please give it a good home.

exchangeItems.title: Kids' bike (ages 5-8)
exchangeItems.description: Our youngest outgrew it. Some scratches but rides perfectly. Happy to swap for anything the kids can use or just give it away.

exchangeItems.title: Indoor plants (x4)
exchangeItems.description: Moving to a darker apartment. These beauties need sunlight and someone who'll love them. Pothos, peace lily, spider plant, and a snake plant.

exchangeItems.title: Board games collection (8 games)
exchangeItems.description: Pandemic, Catan, Ticket to Ride and more. Kids prefer screens now... Free to a family who still plays together!

communities.name: Zero Waste Fitzroy
communities.description: Local community for zero waste living and swap events in Fitzroy. We meet monthly to swap goods, share tips, and keep usable items out of landfill.
communities.recentActivity: 87 items found new homes at Saturday's swap!

communities.name: Repair Cafe Brunswick
communities.description: Share repair skills and fix items together instead of throwing them away. All skill levels welcome — sewing, soldering, woodwork, bike repair.

communities.name: Minimalists Together
communities.description: A supportive space for people on their decluttering journey. No judgement, no pressure — just neighbours helping neighbours let go and feel lighter.

communities.name: Slow Fashion Collingwood
communities.description: For those who love style but refuse fast fashion. Swap clothes, share styling tips, and celebrate second-hand finds over new purchases.
communities.recentActivity: Clothing swap this weekend — 63 signed up!

communities.name: Backyard Growers
communities.description: Growing your own food, composting, and sharing the harvest. Connect with urban gardeners across the inner north and swap seeds, seedlings, and produce.

communities.name: Second Life Electronics
communities.description: Giving electronics a second life through repair, upcycling, and responsible recycling. If it's broken, we'll try to fix it before it hits the bin.

communities.name: Neighbourhood Exchange
communities.description: Hyperlocal sharing: tools, kitchen gadgets, books, anything your neighbour might need. Why buy when you can borrow from someone down the street?

communities.name: Conscious Consumers
communities.description: A space for anyone working toward more intentional living. Share wins, ask for advice, and find accountability partners on the journey to less.

communityPosts.content: Just finished our monthly swap — 87 items found new homes! A woman brought her elderly mum along and she left with a beautiful coat she'd been needing all winter. This is what it's about.
I gave away my late husband's record collection today. I was terrified, but the young couple who took them were so excited. They promised to play them. Somehow that makes it okay.
Borrowed a pressure washer from the tool library, cleaned our whole driveway, returned it in 3 hours. Why did I ever think I needed to own one?
I swapped a dress I never wore for the most beautiful hand-knitted scarf. The woman who made it told me the story behind the pattern. You don't get that on eBay.
Tomato seedlings are ready to share — about 20 extras. My neighbour's kids helped me pot them. Come grab some from my front porch!
Brought my broken toaster to the repair cafe. Not only did they fix it in 20 minutes, but the volunteer taught me how to do it myself next time. Left feeling like I actually learned something.
Week 3 of not buying anything new for the kids. Honestly? They haven't even noticed. All the toys they needed were already on Unclutter. The hardest part was letting go of my own shopping habit.
When I moved to Melbourne I didn't know anyone. Joining this community changed everything. I furnished my whole apartment from neighbours' giveaways, and now those people are my friends.

I'm 72 and living alone since Jim passed. Giving things away through Unclutter has been the best thing for me — not because I needed the space, but because every pickup is a conversation with a neighbour I never would have met.

My daughter asked why we give things to strangers. I told her they're not strangers — they're neighbours we haven't met yet. She's now 'donating' her old toys with handwritten notes for the new owners.

events.title: Fitzroy Community Swap Meet
events.description: Bring items you no longer need and take home something you love. No money, no haggling — just neighbours sharing with neighbours. Tea and coffee provided.

events.title: Repair Cafe: Small Electronics
events.description: Bring your broken toasters, lamps, and gadgets. Volunteer fixers will help you repair them for free. Learn a skill, save an item from landfill, meet your neighbours.

events.title: Clothing Swap Party
events.description: Refresh your wardrobe without buying new. Bring clean, wearable clothes and swap freely. Styling tips and alterations help on the day. BYO tote bag!

events.title: Mending & Sewing Workshop
events.description: Learn to patch, darn, and hem. Bring one item to fix — all materials and guidance provided. No experience needed. Just come and learn together.

events.title: Community Seed & Produce Share
events.description: Share seeds, seedlings, herbs, and homegrown produce with your neighbours. Bring what you have, take what you need. Kids welcome!

events.title: Declutter Together: Group Session
events.description: A guided group session where we sort through items together. Supportive, no-judgement environment. Bring a bag of things you're unsure about — we'll figure it out as a group.

hubLocations.name: Fitzroy Community Drop-off
hubLocations.description: Staffed drop-off point accepting clean, usable household items. Items are sorted and made available to neighbours for free.

hubLocations.name: Brunswick Salvos Partnership
hubLocations.description: Salvation Army partner store. Unclutter members get priority drop-off and can request specific items through the platform.
hubLocations.hours: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM

hubLocations.name: Collingwood Repair Cafe
hubLocations.description: Weekly repair sessions for electronics, clothing, and small appliances. Volunteer fixers available every Thursday.

hubLocations.name: Circular Route Van - Fitzroy North
hubLocations.description: Mobile collection service. The van stops here every Wednesday. Leave items at the collection point by 9 AM — we'll sort and redistribute them.
hubLocations.address: Edinburgh Gardens Car Park, Fitzroy North VIC 3068

hubLocations.name: Circular Route Van - Brunswick East
hubLocations.description: Mobile collection service. The van stops here every Friday morning. No appointment needed — just drop and go.
hubLocations.address: Lygon St & Albion St, Brunswick East VIC 3057

hubLocations.name: Vinnies Collingwood
hubLocations.description: St Vincent de Paul partner store. Accepts quality donations and provides affordable goods to the community.

hubLocations.name: Fitzroy Library Book Exchange
hubLocations.description: Dedicated book exchange shelf at the library. Take a book, leave a book. All genres welcome.
hubLocations.hours: Library hours: Mon-Thu 10 AM - 8 PM, Fri-Sun 10 AM - 5 PM
